codeReview.summary: Implementation adds a storm-candidate ingestion path and tests, but it does not correctly satisfy county/area-targeted generation for VAL-EXPAND-003 in production behavior.

issues.description: `build_storm_leads_from_candidates()` sends Broward/Palm Beach ZIP lists into `fetch_parcels_by_zip()`, but that scraper is hardwired to the Miami-Dade GeoProp endpoint. High-scoring non-Miami candidates therefore cannot reliably generate leads from the intended county source, violating the storm-to-lead generation assertion for county-targeted scans. Evidence: `pipeline/leads.py:537` calls `fetch_parcels_by_zip(county_zips, ...)`; `scrapers/parcels.py:10-12` defines only Miami-Dade `PARCEL_URL`.

The scan is county-wide only and does not use candidate area specificity (`city`/`zip`) when present. Requirement calls for targeted scan by county/area; current code always scans full county ZIP sets (`_COUNTY_ZIPS`) and ignores candidate-level area filters.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `python-backend` skill recommends dry-running real scraper APIs for manual verification, while mission AGENTS prohibits real external scraper calls during tests. This mismatch can push workers into procedural ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-backend/SKILL.md step 6 vs mission AGENTS.md 'DO NOT make real API calls to external scrapers during tests — always mock HTTP'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state does not explicitly map how county expansion should be handled when parcel ingestion is Miami-Dade-only at scraper level, which contributed to an implementation that routes all counties through the Miami endpoint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md notes parcels.py as Miami-Dade parcel layer; commit e0cf241/pipeline changes add Broward/Palm ZIP routing without adding county-specific parcel clients.

summary: Fail: storm-candidate wiring and tests were added, but county/area targeting is not correctly implemented. Non-Miami candidates are routed through a Miami-only parcel API, so VAL-EXPAND-003 is not fully met.
